page.title: San Juan Yacht Charter in Marbella: Celebrate the Summer Solstice at Sea

data.meta_description: Book a San Juan yacht charter in Marbella for the summer solstice. Celebrate with drinks, snacks & skipper from €749. Depart Puerto Banús. Reserve now.
data.summary: Celebrate the San Juan summer solstice aboard a private yacht from Puerto Banús. Our fleet includes 13+ boats with licensed skipper, drinks, and snacks included.
key_facts: San Juan yacht charter Marbella from €749/2h (Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39).
All charters include licensed skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, insurance & IVA.
Mangusta 80 charters (4h min) include a free jet ski – perfect for solstice fun.
Depart from Puerto Banús; anchor at Cala del Faro or Cala Cortés for bonfires.
Boats available for groups of 5 to 12 passengers; contact us for larger parties.
Pricing on request via WhatsApp for all boats except Tier A & Mangusta 80.

faq.q: What is included in a San Juan yacht charter in Marbella?
faq.a: Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). You just bring your swimsuit and sunscreen.

faq.q: How much does a San Juan yacht charter cost?
faq.a: Our Tier A boats (Astondoa 40, Azimut 39) cost €749 for 2 hours and €2,299 for 8 hours. Mangusta 80 charters start at €4,719 for a minimum of 4 hours and include a free jet ski. Other boats are priced on request via WhatsApp.

faq.q: Which boats are available for San Juan night?
faq.a: We offer over 13 boats from Puerto Banús, including the Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax), Azimut 58 Flybridge (17.6m, 12 pax), and speedboats for smaller groups. See our full fleet on the website.

faq.q: Can we watch the San Juan bonfires from the yacht?
faq.a: Absolutely. We recommend anchoring near Cala del Faro, Cala Cortés, or along the Marbella coastline. From the water you'll have front-row views of the bonfires and fireworks without the crowds.

faq.q: How do I book a San Juan yacht charter in Marbella?
faq.a: Contact us via WhatsApp for availability and pricing. For Tier A boats and Mangusta 80, you can request a direct booking link. We recommend booking at least two weeks in advance for San Juan.

faq.q: What time should we depart for San Juan?
faq.a: Most guests depart around 18:00–19:00 to enjoy sunset at sea, then anchor for the bonfires from 22:00 onwards. Charters can run until midnight or later depending on the booking duration.

faq.q: Is the skipper included in the charter?
faq.a: Yes, a licensed skipper is included in every charter. You don't need a boating license. The skipper will navigate, anchor, and ensure your safety throughout the trip.

faq.q: Can we bring our own food and decorations?
faq.a: Yes, you are welcome to bring your own food, drinks, and decorations (e.g., lanterns, banners). Just let us know in advance so we can accommodate. Glass bottles are discouraged for safety.

data.body_html:
San Juan (Saint John's Eve) is one of Spain's most magical nights — a celebration of the summer solstice with bonfires, fireworks, and midnight dips in the sea. A San Juan yacht charter in Marbella lets you experience this tradition from the best seat in the house: your own private boat on the Mediterranean. Depart from Puerto Banús, anchor off a quiet cove, and watch the coastline glow as the sun sets around 21:45. With a licensed skipper, drinks, and snacks included, you just bring the party spirit.

Why celebrate San Juan on a yacht?

Marbella's beaches draw thousands for San Juan, but the water stays blissfully uncrowded. On a yacht you skip the packed sand, the queues, and the noise. Instead you get a private deck, a cooler full of cava and beer, and the freedom to anchor wherever the bonfires look best. The water temperature in late June averages 22°C — perfect for a solstice swim under the stars.

Our fleet for San Juan yacht charter Marbella

We operate from Puerto Banús with over 13 boats ranging from 6.4m speedboats to 29m superyachts. All charters include a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). Below are the most popular choices for San Juan night.

BoatLengthMax paxPrice from
Astondoa 4012.5m9€749/2h
Azimut 3912.5m11€749/2h
Mangusta 8024m12€4,719/4h
Azimut 58 Flybridge17.6m12On request
Speedboat7m6On request

For the full list, visit our boat rental Marbella page. All boats depart from Puerto Banús.

Best spots to anchor for San Juan bonfires

The Marbella coastline offers several coves where San Juan bonfires are traditionally lit. Cala del Faro, just west of Puerto Banús, is a favourite — small, rocky, and dramatic at night. Cala Cortés near Cabopino also sees bonfires, with less boat traffic. For a livelier scene, anchor off the Golden Mile or near the Marbella Marina where organised fireworks displays run until midnight. Your skipper will know the best anchorages based on wind and swell.

What to expect on San Juan night

Spaniards celebrate San Juan with bonfires, jumping over flames (for good luck), and writing wishes on paper to burn at midnight. On a yacht you can join in by lighting a small lantern (check with your skipper) or simply floating under the stars. Many guests bring glow sticks, portable speakers, and a change of dry clothes. The night stays warm — around 20°C even at midnight — so a light jacket is enough.

How to book your San Juan yacht charter in Marbella

San Juan is one of the busiest nights of the year. We recommend booking at least two weeks in advance. For Tier A boats (Astondoa 40, Azimut 39) and Mangusta 80, prices are fixed and listed above. For all other boats — including the Ferretti 94, Canados 86, or Maiora 26m — contact us via WhatsApp for availability and pricing. We'll confirm within hours.

Extend your evening: sunset cruise + San Juan

Many guests combine a sunset cruise (18:00–20:30) with the San Juan festivities (22:00–00:00). A 6-hour charter gives you both: watch the sun dip behind La Concha mountain, then motor to a cove for the bonfires. The Azimut 58 Flybridge is ideal for this — its flybridge offers panoramic views, and the 17.6m deck has plenty of space for your group of up to 12.

Frequently asked questions

What is included in a San Juan yacht charter in Marbella?

Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). You just bring your swimsuit and sunscreen.

How much does a San Juan yacht charter cost?

Our Tier A boats (Astondoa 40, Azimut 39) cost €749 for 2 hours and €2,299 for 8 hours. Mangusta 80 charters start at €4,719 for a minimum of 4 hours and include a free jet ski. Other boats are priced on request via WhatsApp.

Which boats are available for San Juan night?

We offer over 13 boats from Puerto Banús, including the Mangusta 80 (24m, 12 pax), Azimut 58 Flybridge (17.6m, 12 pax), and speedboats for smaller groups. See our full fleet on the website.

Can we watch the San Juan bonfires from the yacht?

Absolutely. We recommend anchoring near Cala del Faro, Cala Cortés, or along the Marbella coastline. From the water you'll have front-row views of the bonfires and fireworks without the crowds.

How do I book a San Juan yacht charter in Marbella?

Contact us via WhatsApp for availability and pricing. For Tier A boats and Mangusta 80, you can request a direct booking link. We recommend booking at least two weeks in advance for San Juan.

What time should we depart for San Juan?

Most guests depart around 18:00–19:00 to enjoy sunset at sea, then anchor for the bonfires from 22:00 onwards. Charters can run until midnight or later depending on the booking duration.

Is the skipper included in the charter?

Yes, a licensed skipper is included in every charter. You don't need a boating license. The skipper will navigate, anchor, and ensure your safety throughout the trip.

Can we bring our own food and decorations?

Yes, you are welcome to bring your own food, drinks, and decorations (e.g., lanterns, banners). Just let us know in advance so we can accommodate. Glass bottles are discouraged for safety.
